content.content: From Baneshwor Chowk west via Everest Hotel up to Maitighar Mandala,
From Baneshwor Chowk east via Minbhawan and Shantinagar up to Tinkune Chowk,
From Baneshwor Chowk north via Eyeplex Mall up to Ratna Rajya Secondary School,
From Baneshwor Chowk south via Shankhamul slope up to Shankhamul Bridge.

Due to unruly and undesirable activities during protests centered in the Baneshwor, Maitighar, and Singha Durbar areas on 2082/05/23, which disrupted peace and security, a request is made to the Nepalese Army (Narasingh Dal Battalion, Singha Durbar Barracks) to provide necessary assistance in implementing the curfew order issued by the Chief District Officer as per Section 6, Sub-section (2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

Considering the chaotic situation including clashes, confrontations, riots, and unrest during the protests by various groups in Kathmandu on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to the Chief District Officer to issue a curfew order as per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, for the following areas effective from 12:00 midnight on 2082/05/23 until 5:00 AM on 2082/05/24, based on the recommendation of the District Security Committee Kathmandu.
Shital Niwas (President's Office) Maharajgunj Area:
North-East: Up to the intersection in front of the National Police Academy,
East-South: 100 meters south from the turn leading to Samakhushi,
South-West: From the turn leading to Samakhushi up to the Samakhushi River.
Harit Griha (Vice President's Office) Lainchaur Area:
From Keshar Mahal Chowk through Lainchaur turn up to the Malla Hotel boundary.
Narayanhiti Palace Museum Area:
From Keshar Mahal Chowk through the Narayanhiti Palace Museum South Gate up to Jai Nepal turn,
From the Narayanhiti Palace Museum South Gate up to the Mahendra Statue.
Singha Durbar Area:
From the eastern corner of Bhadrakali Temple (Tinkune Island) east to the Singha Durbar Main Gate,
From the front of Singha Durbar Main Gate north to the turn of Panchodaya Secondary School,

From Panchodaya turn east past the Office of the Auditor General to the north-eastern turn of Singha Durbar leading to Anamnagar, and from that turn through the Singha Durbar East Gate up to Hanumansthan Temple,
From the front of Singha Durbar Main Gate south past the Supreme Court up to the Department of Archaeology intersection,
From Maitighar Mandala west up to Thapathali Chowk,
From the Department of Archaeology intersection through the Singha Durbar South Gate up to Hanumansthan Temple,
From the eastern entrance of the District Court to the northern turn of the District Administration Office.
Prime Minister's Residence Baluwatar Area:
From Baluwatar Pavitra Workshop Chowk through Nepal Rastra Bank to Italia Chowk, and via the Chinese Embassy to the stone spout (Dhungedhara) in front of Lakshmi Bank towards Bhatbhateni,
From Italia Chowk west to the Residence of the Chairperson of the National Assembly and up to 100 meters from said residence towards the road to Gairidhara,
From the north-eastern turn of the Prime Minister's Residence to the Chief Justice's Residence turn and 100 meters west from that turn,
From the Chief Justice's Residence turn south-east to the turn of the National Assembly Chairperson's Residence,
Up to 100 meters on the road heading east from Rastra Bank Chowk.
Parliament House New Baneshwor Area:
From Baneshwor Chowk west via Everest Hotel up to Maitighar Mandala,
From Baneshwor Chowk east via Minbhawan and Shantinagar up to Tinkune Chowk,
From Baneshwor Chowk north via Eyeplex Mall up to Ratna Rajya Secondary School,
From Baneshwor Chowk south via Shankhamul slope up to Shankhamul Bridge.

Due to unruly and undesirable activities during protests centered in the Baneshwor, Maitighar, and Singha Durbar areas on 2082/05/23, which disrupted peace and security, a request is made to the Nepalese Army (Narasingh Dal Battalion, Singha Durbar Barracks) to provide necessary assistance in implementing the curfew order issued by the Chief District Officer as per Section 6, Sub-section (2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

Considering the chaotic situation including clashes, confrontations, riots, and unrest during the protests by various groups in Kathmandu on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to the Chief District Officer to issue a curfew order as per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, for the following areas effective from 8:30 AM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, based on the recommendation of the District Security Committee Kathmandu.
Within the Kathmandu Metropolitan City Ring Road area:

The entire area inside the Ring Road of Kathmandu Metropolitan City, from Balkumari Bridge to Koteshwor Chowk, Sinamangal, Gaushala, Chabahil, Narayan Gopal Chowk, Gongabu, Balaju, Swayambhu, Kalanki, and Balkhu, extending to the Bagmati River Bridge.

In view of the unruly and undesirable activities during demonstrations centered in the Baneshwor, Maitighar, and Singha Durbar areas on 2082/05/23, which disrupted peace and security, a request is made to the Nepalese Army (Narasingh Dal Battalion, Singha Durbar Barracks) to provide necessary assistance in implementing the curfew order issued by the Chief District Officer, as per Section 6, Sub-section (2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

As various groups and parties held demonstrations in the Valley area on 2082/05/23, which could lead to violent activities, riots, or unrest and affect public peace and security, a request is made to Lalitpur Metropolitan City, Mahalaxmi Municipality, and Godawari Municipality to close all schools/colleges and educational institutions for 2 (two) days on Bhadra 24 and 25, in accordance with the Local Administration Act, 1971. Additionally, the District Administration Office shall publish a notice to this effect.

Information has been received from various sources that groups including Gen-Z and other youths are preparing to hold demonstrations in Kathmandu Valley from 2082 Bhadra 23. Therefore, all security agencies shall assess potential security risks, make necessary preparations for reliable security arrangements, and exchange information regularly.

In view of the demonstrations and to protect public property and lives, special alertness shall be maintained in risk-prone areas (VA/VP), including the Ministers' Residence in Bhaisepati, Judges' Residence in Pulchowk, Nepali Congress Central Office, CPN (UML) Central Office, Balkumari Bridge, telecommunication service providers/infrastructure, and other important installations and government offices. Security agencies shall deploy personnel coordinately based on an integrated security plan.
Reviewed the progress of implementation of previous District Security Committee decisions. The peace and security situation of the district has remained normal thus far.
As demonstrations by various groups on 2082/05/23 may lead to violence or unrest, it is recommended to declare the following areas as prohibited zones:
Area within 200 meters of the UN House and Judges' Residence in Pulchowk:
East: The main road leading to Inarmode,
West: National Records Office (Civil),
North: Inland Revenue Office,
South: National Human Rights Commission and National Cooperative Development Board premises.
The area of the new Ministers' Residence in Bhaisepati:
East: The road from Bhaisepati to Bungamati,
West: CG Housing,
North: Bhaisepati Residential Gate,
South: The descent leading to Itani.
The area of the Nepali Congress Central Office in Sanepa:
East: The paved road from Sanepa Chowk to Indrayani Temple,
West: Bagmati Corridor road from Balkhu Bridge to Manmohan Park,
North: Service lane of the Ring Road (from Balkhu Bridge to Sanepa Chowk),
South: The paved road from Indrayani Temple to the Bagmati Corridor.
The area of the CPN (UML) Central Office in Chyasal:
East: Suspension bridge over the Manohara River corridor,
West: Chyasal Stadium,
North: Manohara River,
South: The road leading to Mal Pokhari.
Due to the potential for violence and unrest arising from demonstrations in the Valley on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to declare the following areas as prohibited zones:
Areas in Ward No. 5, 13, and 14 of Lalitpur Metropolitan City:
From Mahalaxmi Chowk to Patan Hospital,
From Patan Hospital to Jawalakhel Chowk,
From Jawalakhel Chowk to Ekantakuna,
From Ekantakuna to Mahalaxmi Chowk.

It is recommended to issue a curfew order effective from 9:00 AM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing are prohibited within the following boundaries of Lalitpur Metropolitan City, Lalitpur district.

content: The entire area inside and including the Ring Road of Lalitpur Metropolitan City, from Balkhu, Dhobighat, Sanepa, Ekantakuna, Mahalaxmisthan, Satdobato, and Gwarko to Balkumari Bridge.

content.content: Due to potential violent activities and unrest from demonstrations on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to issue a curfew order within the specified four boundaries.

A curfew order shall be issued from 7:00 AM to 12:00 midnight on 2082/05/24, under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, prohibiting movement, assembly, processions, or meetings within the following boundaries of Wards 2, 4, 9, 18, and 25 of Lalitpur Metropolitan City.
